lorehq

─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ AI Review Gate ]─ ┐

AI Review Gate

GitHub Actions workflow that runs AI-powered architecture review on field-fix PRs. Posts a structured review comment with pass/fail per criterion before human review.

Goal

Every field-fix PR receives an automated convention and architecture review, catching common issues (security leaks, cross-platform breakage, scope violations) before a human maintainer spends time reviewing.

Review Criteria

The AI review evaluates each PR against these criteria and posts a structured comment:

CriterionSourcePass/Fail
Coding conventionsdocs/context/conventions/coding.mdRequired
Security conventionsdocs/context/conventions/security.mdRequired
Cross-platform bash compatibilitybash-macos-compat skill knowledgeRequired
Cross-tool impactDoes change affect Cursor/OpenCode/Claude Code?Advisory
Ownership boundariesOnly harness-owned files modifiedRequired
MinimalityChanges only what’s needed, no unrelated modificationsRequired
Derived file consistencyPlatform copies, CLAUDE.md, cursor rules stay in syncAdvisory
Test evidenceSubmission includes verification of the fixAdvisory

Required criteria block merge if they fail. Advisory criteria are flagged but don’t block.

Review Comment Format

## Field Fix Review

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Coding conventions | PASS | — |
| Security conventions | PASS | — |
| Cross-platform compatibility | FAIL | Uses `sed -i ''` (macOS-only) |
| Cross-tool impact | INFO | Affects Claude Code hooks only |
| Ownership boundaries | PASS | — |
| Minimality | PASS | — |
| Derived file consistency | WARN | Platform copies may need updating |
| Test evidence | PASS | Before/after output provided |

### Summary
1 blocking issue found. Fix cross-platform sed usage before merge.

Implementation

  • Workflow: .github/workflows/field-fix-review.yml
  • Trigger: pull_request: [opened, synchronize] with field-fix label
  • Tool: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 with a review prompt that references the convention files
  • Prompt template: .github/prompts/field-fix-review.md — instructs the AI to evaluate against each criterion and output the structured table

Scope

Target repo: /home/andrew/Github/lore (source framework)

Files to create:

  • .github/workflows/field-fix-review.yml — the Actions workflow
  • .github/prompts/field-fix-review.md — prompt template for the review

Dependencies

  • Phase 3 (intake triage) — review fires after triage labels the PR as field-fix
  • Phase 1 (metadata format) — review reads the YAML metadata block from the PR body

Deliverables

  • GitHub Actions workflow triggers on field-fix PRs
  • AI review posts a structured comment with per-criterion pass/fail
  • Blocking criteria are clearly distinguished from advisory
  • Review runs against the actual diff, not just the metadata
  • Updated PRs (force-push, new commits) trigger re-review

Success Criteria

  • A PR with a macOS-only sed command gets flagged on cross-platform compatibility
  • A PR modifying files outside harness-owned directories gets flagged on ownership
  • A PR with instance-specific paths in the diff gets flagged on security
  • A clean, minimal, cross-platform fix passes all required criteria
  • Review comment is posted within 3 minutes of PR creation
  • Re-review on updated PRs replaces the previous review comment (no comment spam)

Test Plan

# Create a test PR with a known cross-platform issue
# Verify review comment appears with FAIL on compatibility

# Create a clean test PR
# Verify review comment appears with all PASS

# Push a fix commit to the failing PR
# Verify re-review replaces the old comment
